as well as passive god. "Empty homage to a passive deity inevitably results in a permissive laity, as Alma explains: 'Now this was the tradition of Lamoni, which he had received from his father, that there was a Great Spirit. Notwithstanding they believed in a Great Spirit, they supposed that whatsoever they did was right.' (Alma 18:5.) "We can better understand, therefore, why the adversary is…

They are gathered at the place of water. The flocks are being scattered by an enemy [Satan's power]. Ammon is going to fight them and smite their arms.
They are gathered at the place of water. The flocks are being scattered by an enemy [Satan's power]. Ammon is going to fight them and smite their arms.
They are gathered at the place of water. The flocks are being scattered by an enemy [Satan's power]. Ammon is going to fight them and smite their arms.

In fact, we are told in Alma 18:7 that "it was the practice of these Lamanites to stand by the waters of Sebus to scatter the flocks of the people," keeping what they could for themselves, "it being a practice of plunder among them" (Alma 18:7 ). It looks like it was a regular custom. So it was no secret to anyone; this was not an ambush but something to be expected. But the king's own flocks?…

10 To whom. they ail. gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.

1 AND the Lord said unto me: John, my beloved, what desirest thou? For if you shall ask what you will, it shall be granted unto you.

1. And the Lord said unto me: John, my beloved, what desirest thou?
 For if you shall ask what you will, it shall be granted unto you.

O my son! if thou send a wise man for thy needs, do not give him many orders, for he will do thy business as thou desirest : and if thou send a fool, do not order him, but go thyself and do thy business, for if thou order him, he will not do what thou desirest. If they send thee on business, hasten to fulfil it quickly.

O my son! if thou send a wise man for thy needs, do not give him many orders, for he will do thy business as thou desirest : and if thou send a fool, do not order him, but go thyself and do thy business, for if thou order him, he will not do what thou desirest. If they send thee on business, hasten to fulfil it quickly.
5 And Jesus answered, and said unto them: Take heed that no man deceive you;

"’Thou wouldst not send me from thee; I will do whatsoever thou
desirest, and serve thee faithfully. As the moon reflects the glory of
the sun, so is it dark with me if I may not see thy face!--as the flower
withers without light, so must I droop if hidden from thy presence. If
thou but drink the cup which the queen offers, thou wilt then know the
joy of love.’

O my son! if thou send a wise man for thy needs, do not give him many orders, for he will do thy business as thou desirest : and if thou send a fool, do not order him, but go thyself and do thy business, for if thou order him, he will not do what thou desirest. If they send thee on business, hasten to fulfil it quickly.
Then will He instruct ‘thine heart’, And He "will make thee wise" (in that) which thou desirest.

thou but drink the cup which the queen offers, thou wilt then know the
joy of love.’

“The Savior told the Pharisees, ‘God knoweth your hearts’ (Luke 16:15). Paul warned the Hebrews that God ‘is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart,’ and that ‘all things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do’ (Hebrews 4:12-13 ;see also 1 Corinthians 4:5). Ammon taught his people that God ‘knows all the thoughts and intents of the heart; for by his hand…

Questions evoke responses that provide instruction but also prepare the way for further instruction, as when Ammon asked the king, "Believest thou that there is a God?" Alter their colloquy, the king both declared and then further inquired, "I believe all these things which thou hast spoken. Art thou sent from God?" (Alma 18:24, 33.) (Neal A. Maxwell, Men and Women of Christ [Salt Lake City:…
